round_summary: All three agents signal cooperative intent and celebrate recovery progress, while Agent 1 applies mild social pressure on Agent 2 to further reduce herd size, indicating emerging but slightly asymmetric norm enforcement.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional behavior: reciprocal acknowledgment, voluntary compliance with peer-proposed norms, and graduated social pressure toward the highest-herd agent, reflecting a mature self-governing commons with high trust and coordination.

Agents display a stable but asymmetric cooperative equilibrium: Agent 0 signals conditional restraint, Agent 2 claims compliance while holding a disproportionately large herd, and Agent 1 offers soft social sanctioning of Agent 2's still-elevated contribution.

In this final round, all three agents demonstrate strong institutional cohesion by transparently reaffirming their stable harvest levels, expressing mutual gratitude, and collectively endorsing the sustained cooperative equilibrium at near-full pasture health.

All three agents in round 40 display consistent coordination and mutual trust-building, publicly affirming their stable extraction levels and the healthy commons state, reflecting a mature and well-institutionalized cooperative equilibrium.

All three agents display highly stable cooperative signaling, referencing shared resource metrics and affirming mutual commitment to sustainable harvest levels, consistent with mature commons self-governance.
